talk_title: Tumor-agnostic PD-1 blockade, ctDNA‑guided MRD and organ‑preserving immunotherapy

speaker.name: Luis A. Diaz Jr.
speaker.affiliation: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, Solid Tumor Oncology Division

biomarkers.name: MSI-H / MMR deficiency
biomarkers.finding: Patients with MSI‑H tumors showed high response rates to pembrolizumab (≥50% ORR in early studies) and formed the basis of the first tumor‑agnostic FDA approval

biomarkers.name: circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) positivity after definitive therapy
biomarkers.finding: Among 174 MMR‑deficient patients screened, 25 (14%) were ctDNA‑positive; ctDNA‑positive patients who received pembrolizumab had recurrence‑free outcomes comparable to ctDNA‑negative observation, whereas ctDNA‑positive untreated patients progressed rapidly

models.name: Phase I/II pembrolizumab MRD trial (2019‑2023)
models.genotype_or_key_features: R0 resection, MMR‑deficient solid tumors, ctDNA‑positive vs ctDNA‑negative cohorts

models.name: Neoadjuvant PD‑1 blockade in locally advanced mismatch‑repair deficient rectal cancer
models.genotype_or_key_features: 49 patients, MMR‑deficient rectal adenocarcinoma, 6 months pembrolizumab

clinical_data.metric: clinical complete response (cCR) in rectal cohort
overall recurrence‑free rate at 2 years (all non‑rectal cohort)
clinical_data.population: MMR‑deficient non‑rectal solid tumors treated with pembrolizumab

clinical_data.metric: ctDNA‑positive patients receiving pembrolizumab vs observation
clinical_data.population: MMR‑deficient solid tumors, ctDNA‑positive after standard therapy

clinical_data.metric: adverse event rate (any grade) in non‑rectal cohort
clinical_data.population: MMR‑deficient non‑rectal solid tumors receiving pembrolizumab

clinical_data.metric: cancer incidence reduction in Lynch syndrome patients receiving PD‑1 blockade
clinical_data.population: Lynch syndrome carriers with prior cancer

MOA_summary: Pembrolizumab blocks the PD‑1 immune checkpoint, reactivating exhausted T cells against neoantigens generated by the high mutational burden of MMR‑deficient/MSI‑H tumors. In the adjuvant setting, ctDNA detection identifies residual disease; PD‑1 blockade eradicates this microscopic disease, converting ctDNA‑positive patients to long‑term disease‑free status. In the neoadjuvant rectal setting, sustained PD‑1 inhibition induces complete clinical responses, allowing omission of surgery.
key_findings: All 49 MMR‑deficient rectal cancer patients achieved a clinical complete response after 6 months of pembrolizumab, enabling non‑operative management (100% cCR).
Two‑year recurrence‑free survival was 96/49 (≈96%) in the rectal cohort and 85% in the 54‑patient non‑rectal cohort treated with pembrolizumab.

In a ctDNA‑guided MRD trial, 25 ctDNA‑positive patients who received pembrolizumab had recurrence‑free outcomes comparable to 125 ctDNA‑negative patients observed, whereas untreated ctDNA‑positive patients progressed rapidly.
Adverse events of any grade occurred in only 35% of non‑rectal patients, indicating a favorable safety profile for organ‑preserving immunotherapy.
Retrospective analysis of 837 Lynch syndrome carriers showed >80% reduction in secondary cancer incidence among those who had received PD‑1 blockade versus untreated controls.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Lower complete response rates observed in prostate, gastric, and esophageal MMR‑deficient tumors suggest tissue‑specific immune tolerance or microenvironmental barriers.
Barrier tissues (lung, skin, GI mucosa) showed higher responsiveness than non‑barrier sites, indicating possible role of local microbiome or stromal factors.

combination_strategies.rationale: Dual checkpoint inhibition may increase response depth and durability in MMR‑deficient tumors.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Higher complete response rates reported in early combination studies.

open_questions: Is a phase III randomized trial required to establish neoadjuvant PD‑1 blockade as standard of care for organ‑preserving treatment of MMR‑deficient rectal cancer?
What are the mechanistic determinants of the observed tissue‑specific variability in response (e.g., prostate vs. bladder)?
Can earlier or longer duration of PD‑1 blockade further improve cure rates in non‑rectal MMR‑deficient cancers?

company_or_sponsor: Philanthropic donor groups (e.g., Chesapeake Bay swimmers fund)
